research_findings: The current state model has durable session and validation data but no machine-readable slot for research or committee conclusions.
The reporting flow currently requires committee findings to be retyped into write-report flags, which risks drift from the actual scope decision.
validate-kit can exercise a new capture script without mutating state by using a no-state path.
committee_feedback: Product manager committee wants review conclusions stored once and reused, so scope rationale does not get lost between select, implement, and report.
Technical architect committee wants the review artifact to stay small and JSON-shaped, not a long transcript or free-form blob.
User committee wants future operators to understand what was challenged without scanning old chat logs or hand-written notes.
committee_decision: Add a concise review_state object to loop state and a capture-review.py script that records research, committee feedback, and final decisions.
review_state.goal_title: Persist research and committee review conclusions into loop state

results.name: validate-kit

history.goal: Add a real repository validation script and use it as the publish gate
Fix publish ordering so loop state and backlog updates are committed in the same version
Add a reference that defines what high-quality project data means for the loop
Add a structured schema for project data snapshots and evidence
Add a script that collects first-pass project data into a snapshot file
Add a script that scores whether the collected project data is good enough to act on
Integrate data snapshot and quality state into the loop's durable state model
Extend reports to include evidence sources and data quality observations
Improve next-goal selection using data quality gaps and evidence gaps
Align wrappers, protocol, README, and examples around high-quality project data acquisition
Integrate the committee-driven loop model across config, scripts, templates, and references
Persist research and committee review conclusions into loop state
